decisions.title: Start with a bounded Search Intelligence thesis
decisions.context: The venture studio needs one first direction to avoid portfolio drift. The user has unusually strong ecommerce search, PLP, recommendations, and relevance experience, and the strategy file states that a concierge audit can validate revenue before broad automation.
decisions.decision: Use Search Intelligence as the first thesis while keeping it unvalidated until external willingness to pay is observed.
evidence: forge-core/STRATEGY.md: Phase 1 revenue validation exit criteria
alternativesConsidered: Build a generic autonomous app portfolio
Create a commerce test store first
Build a full SaaS platform before sales validation
decisions.expectedValue: High, because the thesis has founder-domain fit and can be tested manually before large product investment.
decisions.risk: Medium, because willingness to pay is not validated yet.
decisions.reversibility: High until money is spent, customers are promised outcomes, or substantial product code is built.
decisions.approvalGate: No spending, outreach, customer charging, or published customer claims without human approval.

decisions.title: Complete Phase 0 core ledgers before revenue work
decisions.context: The first automation run found strategy and governance text but no decision ledger, opportunity ledger, experiment ledger, deterministic validation, or next-run handoff. Governance requires future runs to read these files before selecting work.
decisions.decision: Implement the missing Forge Core repository state as the single coherent increment for this run.
evidence: forge-core/GOVERNANCE.md: anti-drift controls require ledgers and handoff
forge-core/STRATEGY.md: Phase 0 exit criteria require core state validation
alternativesConsidered: Draft the first Search Intelligence sales offer
decisions.expectedValue: High, because durable state and validation reduce strategic drift across all future runs.
decisions.risk: Low, because the change is documentation and deterministic validation only.
decisions.reversibility: High, because files can be revised by pull request.
decisions.approvalGate: No approval gate triggered. No spending, outreach, customer data, legal commitment, or pricing action occurs.
